note: Adminimal Admin Toolbar is a CSS/library layer that restyles the Admin Toolbar; it has one boolean setting and a hard dependency on admin_toolbar. All cases are `recipe` (response-graded), probing documented facts (what it does, its dependency, its single config key, its libraries) rather than live site state.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site, what does the adminimal_admin_toolbar module do, and does it add any new toolbar features or menu links?
evals.expected_output: It restyles the admin toolbar with the minimalist 'Adminimal' dark look-and-feel. It is a pure CSS/styling layer and adds no new toolbar features or menu links.

evals.prompt: Which contrib module must be installed for adminimal_admin_toolbar to work, and what core module does that pull in?
evals.expected_output: It depends on the contrib admin_toolbar module (declared admin_toolbar:admin_toolbar in info.yml), which in turn depends on core's Toolbar (toolbar) module.

evals.prompt: Is adminimal_admin_toolbar a feature module or a theme/CSS layer? How does it apply its styling to the toolbar?
evals.expected_output: It is a CSS/theme layer, not a feature module. It attaches CSS libraries via hook_page_attachments_alter() and adds an 'adminimal-admin-toolbar' body class (only for users with the 'access toolbar' permission) that scopes the styles.

evals.prompt: How do I install and enable adminimal_admin_toolbar on a Drupal 11 site, and is there anything else to configure to make the styling appear?
evals.expected_output: composer require drupal/adminimal_admin_toolbar then drush en adminimal_admin_toolbar -y. Simply enabling it applies the styling; no further configuration is required.

evals.prompt: Does adminimal_admin_toolbar have any configuration? If so, what is the one setting and which config object holds it?
evals.expected_output: Yes, one setting: avoid_custom_font (boolean, default FALSE) on the adminimal_admin_toolbar.settings config object. When TRUE it skips loading the Open Sans Google font.

evals.prompt: What admin path and route lead to adminimal_admin_toolbar's settings form, and which permission is required?
evals.expected_output: The form is at /admin/config/user-interface/adminimal_admin_toolbar (route adminimal_admin_toolbar.settings), guarded by the core 'administer site configuration' permission.

evals.prompt: Which library does adminimal_admin_toolbar attach for the Open Sans font, and how do I stop that font from loading?
evals.expected_output: The adminimal_admin_toolbar/google-fonts library (css/font.css) loads Open Sans. Set avoid_custom_font to TRUE to stop it from being attached.
